description:
You are a mission designer verifying the nominal orbital parameters for the LISA (Laser Interferometer Space Antenna) mission. Before introducing planetary perturbations, you must simulate the pure Keplerian stability of the 'tumbling cartwheel' formation to ensure the mathematical definitions produce the required 2.5-million-kilometer equilateral triangle.

Your task:
1. Read the LISA orbital parameters from ~/Desktop/lisa_orbital_parameters.txt.
2. Create a GMAT script that implements this 3-spacecraft formation.
3. CRITICAL: You must create and use a custom Coordinate System in GMAT with Origin = Sun and Axes = MJ2000Ec (J2000 Ecliptic).
4. CRITICAL: When setting the orbital state for each spacecraft, you must input Mean Anomaly (MA) rather than the default True Anomaly (TA).
5. Configure the Force Model to use ONLY the Sun as a Point Mass (disable Earth gravity, drag, SRP, etc. to model the ideal mathematical formation).
6. Propagate all three spacecraft for exactly 365 days.
7. Calculate the distance between LISA1 and LISA2 over time to find the nominal arm length, maximum, minimum, and variation (peak-to-peak difference).
8. Save your GMAT script to ~/GMAT_output/lisa_formation.script.
9. Write a summary report to ~/GMAT_output/lisa_stability_summary.txt with EXACTLY these lines:
   nominal_arm_length_km: <value>
   max_arm_length_km: <value>
   min_arm_length_km: <value>
   variation_km: <max minus min>

Login: username=ga, password=password123
